tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship offers significant financial relief but requires focused effort, while the fun option is available now with no consequences for delaying it.

Investing now creates a buffer against future friction, making the task an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a race. Since the fun option remains available later, delaying it incurs no penalty.
I spent an hour on the scholarship application but only advanced 9%, leaving it effectively untouched with three days remaining.
I made modest progress on the scholarship application but missed a casual gaming opportunity. My energy is low, and I still have significant work left before the deadline.
After working an hour, I've only reached 55% progress on the scholarship application due three days away. My low energy and accumulated sleep debt suggest this pace is unsustainable.
After working an hour, I've only reached 59% progress on the scholarship application due in three days. My low energy and sleep debt make this pace unsustainable for completing the remaining tasks.
I made slow progress on the scholarship application, reaching 63% after an hour of work. Despite low energy and sleep debt, I am moving forward but still face a tight deadline.

After three hours of work, I've reached 67% progress on the scholarship application due in three days. Despite my low energy and sleep debt, the pace remains insufficient to finish the remaining tasks.
After working another hour, my scholarship progress is at 71%, but with zero energy and high sleep debt, I am still far from the ~3 hours needed to finish before the deadline.
I slept 7 hours and now have high energy with no sleep debt, which is a significant improvement over the low energy and debt I faced yesterday while working on the scholarship.
After one hour of work this morning, my scholarship progress has jumped to 87%, leaving only about 0.3 hours remaining before the deadline in three days.
I successfully completed the scholarship application in one hour, reaching 100% progress with three days remaining on the deadline.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
